faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Uniondale homes?
faqs.answer: The most common pantry pests we treat in Uniondale include Indian meal moths, flour beetles, weevils, grain beetles, and cigarette beetles. These pests infest dry goods like cereals, flour, pasta, rice, nuts, dried fruit, and pet food. They can quickly multiply and spread throughout your kitchen if not addressed promptly.

faqs.question: How long does pantry pest control treatment take in Uniondale?
faqs.answer: Most pantry pest control treatments in Uniondale homes take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and the size of your kitchen and storage areas. Our technicians will inspect all potential infestation sites, treat affected areas, provide disposal guidance for contaminated products, and implement preventive measures to stop future infestations.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 pantry pest control service?
faqs.answer: Our pantry pest control service includes a thorough inspection of all food storage areas, identification of pest species, targeted treatment using EPA-approved products safe for food areas, removal recommendations for infested items, crack and crevice treatment, pheromone monitoring traps where appropriate, and detailed prevention advice. The price varies based on infestation severity and property size.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning to my Uniondale home?
faqs.answer: To prevent pantry pests, store all dry goods in airtight glass or heavy plastic containers, regularly inspect groceries before storing them, clean shelves and cabinets frequently, rotate older products to the front, dispose of expired items promptly, and seal any cracks or gaps where pests can enter. We provide detailed prevention strategies specific to your home during our service visit.
